88.reason: [Error: Missing return statement when EOF is reached without a word; function should return EOF]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: Missing return statement when EOF is reached without a word; function should return EOF]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  缺少 return EOF; 导致文件结束时函数未返回EOF，主循环无法正确终止
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing return EOF; statement, causing undefined behavior when file ends without a trailing delimiter
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Missing return EOF; statement at the end of the function, causing a compilation error in strict environments and failing to correctly signal end-of-file to the caller.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Function does not handle end-of-file properly when the last word is not followed by a delimiter; also uses manual character checks instead of isalpha/tolower consistently, and may overrun buffer if word exceeds MAXWORD]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Missing handling of EOF when a word is being read; if file ends without delimiter, last word may not be null-terminated or returned properly. Also, using char for fgetc() causes EOF comparison issues because char may be signed/unsigned. Should use int for c.]

83.reason: [Error: When w points to the start of the buffer, w-1 is invalid and causes undefined behavior]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: When w points to the start of the buffer, w-1 is invalid and causes undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  *(w-1) may be invalid if no letters processed yet
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Dereferencing w-1 when w points to buffer start causes undefined behavior; also fails to handle punctuation as word boundaries
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Unsafe pointer dereference *(w-1) when no letters have been read yet; logic fails to correctly identify word boundaries and handle the last word in the file.
